briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the Climate Briefing for March 24, 2026. Today, I will present key trends and challenges based on climate-related news collected domestically and internationally.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Change and Its Impacts
sections.content: The global crisis due to climate change is deepening, manifesting in various forms both domestically and internationally. In Korea, warnings have emerged that climate change-related phenomena such as increasing floods, heatwaves, and forced displacement could lead to mental health crises [2, 29]. Furthermore, the economic impact is growing, with the climate crisis leading to income reduction for the younger generation; specifically, Australia's Generation Alpha is projected to experience a lifetime income loss exceeding $185,000 [23, 129]. Internationally, direct impacts on the natural environment are being confirmed, such as the temporary prohibition of climbing in Peru's Andes region due to climate change, and complex avalanche risks in the Pacific Northwest [12, 132]. The UN has warned that the water crisis is worsening amidst conflict and climate change [139], and a report from Australia indicates that despite environmental improvement efforts, climate change is rapidly accelerating damage to marine ecosystems and wildlife [136]. These phenomena clearly demonstrate that climate change is not merely an environmental issue but a complex crisis spanning society, economy, and security [10, 135].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Climate Change Adaptation and Policies
sections.content: Domestic adaptation efforts to directly respond to the impacts of climate change are actively being pursued. Notably, Gyeongsangbuk-do is investing a total of 560 billion won by 2030 to vigorously promote an 'Fisheries Transformation Action Plan,' which includes discovering new fish species like salmon and yellowtail instead of squid and transitioning species in response to changes in fish populations due to climate change [1, 4, 7, 8, 9, 27, 34]. Jeollanam-do is also implementing disaster countermeasures for rural water resources and agricultural infrastructure in response to climate change [13], while the Chungcheongnam-do Provincial Council is revising related regulations to adapt to changing crop cultivation conditions, and Boeun-gun has commenced the creation of a specialized smart orchard complex [18, 30]. Efforts to strengthen policy foundations continue, including research into post-management measures to ensure the effectiveness of climate change impact assessments [6], and the Korea Institute of Ocean Science and Technology (KIOST) and Pusan National University are strengthening cooperation to respond to the climate change crisis [19, 32]. The Korea Golf Course Management Association has published a white paper on course management in the era of climate crisis, proposing a shift in turf survival strategies [28, 33, 35].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Transition to a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: In Korea, comprehensive efforts to achieve the 2050 carbon neutrality goal are accelerating. Cheongju City is pursuing specific plans to create a carbon-neutral city, setting a target of reducing 258,000 tons of greenhouse gases this year and investing 235.5 billion won [42, 43, 44, 63, 86, 97]. Daegu City and Pocheon City are also reviewing the progress of their carbon neutrality and green growth master plans [41, 52, 58, 66, 83, 92]. Chungnam Province is selecting 14 resident-participatory carbon-neutral villages to promote model dissemination [38, 39, 40, 67, 76, 78, 81, 88], and there have been calls for establishing a compensation system for rural and fishing communities' contributions to carbon neutrality [61, 62]. Corporate ESG management practices are also prominent, with KT&G being recognized as an excellent company in the CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project) climate change and water management sectors for four consecutive years, demonstrating its environmental management capabilities [3, 5, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17, 20, 21, 22, 24, 25, 26]. iM Bank has taken the lead in urban ecosystem preservation and carbon neutrality through its 'One Tree, One Step Towards Carbon Neutrality' campaign [45, 46, 54, 56, 68, 70, 71, 87, 91, 96, 101]. Various local governments and institutions, including Yeungnam University, Haman-gun, Jindo-gun, and Naju City, are continuing carbon neutrality efforts through tree-planting events [37, 51, 69, 73, 74, 79, 93, 94, 98, 99, 102]. Incheon City is considering carbon neutrality from the urban planning stage by reflecting smart and carbon-neutral elements in its district unit plan establishment criteria [50, 55, 59, 60, 64, 84, 100], and in Gumi, a 'Carbon-Neutral Industrial Complex' has begun, accelerating the green transition of industrial complexes [57].

sections.title: ⚡ Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Facing Challenges
sections.content: As the transition to renewable energy accelerates, various developments and challenges are emerging both domestically and internationally. The Taean West Sea area has been additionally designated as an offshore wind power cluster, and the Nakwol Offshore Wind Power Project is scheduled for completion within the year [114, 115, 116, 122, 124]. Southern Power is accelerating the expansion of wind power generation by strengthening safety management for wind power complexes [111]. SL Energy is actively pursuing renewable energy projects and expanding its eco-friendly fuel business [112, 123, 125], while XCF Global is contributing to strengthening renewable energy security through SAF (Sustainable Aviation Fuel) production [119]. Bucheon City Council is laying the groundwork for renewable energy expansion by establishing a basis for promoting new and renewable energy generation projects utilizing public property [103, 120], and technological advancements are continuing, such as the start of electric vehicle and renewable energy linkage tests at the Naju MVDC Demonstration Center [126]. However, the issue of 'safety' in new and renewable energy generation has also been raised, with the Yeongdeok fire incident highlighting the need to directly address the risks of aging wind turbines and other renewable energy facilities [105, 113]. Furthermore, as high oil prices and energy security issues emerge, the importance of solar power, wind power, and ESS (Energy Storage Systems) is further emphasized [121]. Meanwhile, controversies surrounding the energy structure of Ulsan's 'Carbon-Neutral Hydrogen Apartment' and cases of rampant solar power development around Japan's Fukushima nuclear power plant suggest the importance of careful planning and management in the process of introducing renewable energy [36, 128].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, climate change is having a severe impact across all aspects of our society. However, at the same time, active adaptation efforts, the practice of carbon neutrality, and the transition to renewable energy are actively underway both domestically and internationally to overcome these challenges. I would like to emphasize once again the importance of continuous interest and effort to navigate this era of climate crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.

The Yomiuri Shimbun reported on the 24th, based on an analysis of satellite data, that solar panels covering 7.1 square kilometers have been installed on agricultural land in Fukushima Prefecture, Japan, where the Fukushima Nuclear Power Plant is located, since the Great East Japan Earthquake.
